citation.anchor: Template Field Encoding and Instruction Slot Mapping

preconditions: Every defined template receives test-owned no-effect placeholder encodings
inputs: Exact literal matrix of all 32 template values
expected_results: All 24 defined templates are accepted and reach the suffix marker
allowed_results: Legal completion for every defined template
cases.committed_prefix: Accepted placeholders complete before the final marker

oracle.derivation: A test-owned 32-entry literal is compared statically with production and executed for acceptance; this does not independently prove every unit and stop mapping

citation.anchor: The X-unit instructions occupy two instruction slots

preconditions: MLX contains a legal logical L+X instruction
inputs: Two MOVL constants and forward/backward BRL displacements beyond imm21 range
expected_results: Exact 64-bit constants and signed imm60 targets
allowed_results: Only the independently reconstructed values and targets
cases.committed_prefix: L and X commit as one instruction
cases.restart_action: Legal L+X starts at logical slot one

oracle.derivation: Test-local bitfield reconstruction and literal signed displacement arithmetic do not import decoder tables

citation.anchor: Attempts to execute a bundle of template MLX when PSR.ri == 2

preconditions: PSR.ic is enabled for precise interruption collection
inputs: All eight reserved templates and RFI to an MLX bundle with saved RI two
expected_results: Precise Illegal Operation for every illegal template or placement
allowed_results: General Exception vector with exact IIP/IPSR.ri/ISR.ei
required_unchanged_state: MLX destination and illegal bundle effects
fault_priority: Placement is rejected before any instruction effect
cases.restart_action: IIP names the bundle and saved RI/EI remain two

oracle.derivation: The manual-derived reserved set and explicit RFI image determine exact fault state

preconditions: Ignored and reserved fields are made observably nonzero
inputs: X3 ignored L bits and POPCNT reserved r2 under true and false qp
expected_results: Ignored bits execute normally; enabled reserved field faults; false qp suppresses it
allowed_results: Exact legal target or precise Illegal Operation
permitted_state_changes: Legal branch IP or interruption resources
fault_priority: False qp suppresses the reserved field; enabled reserved field faults before effects
cases.committed_prefix: The fault preserves its successful prefix
cases.restart_action: Saved RI identifies the reserved instruction

oracle.derivation: Literal ignored-bit and reserved-field encodings are constructed outside the production decoder

citation.anchor: instruction bundle address contained in IIP, along with the IPSR.ri field

preconditions: Precise collection and selected branch/interrupt controls are enabled
inputs: Sequential completion, taken-branch trap, external interrupt, and RFI
expected_results: Exact target or interrupted IP/RI is exposed and restored
fault_priority: Completed transfer trap attribution and interrupted instruction attribution follow the manual

oracle.derivation: Literal bundle addresses, slot numbers, vector addresses, and markers define the expected transition graph

citation.anchor: instruction bundle address contained in IIP, along with the IPSR.ri field

preconditions: A legal three-slot MII bundle has unique destination markers
inputs: Architectural RI zero, one, and two
expected_results: Only the selected slot and its suffix execute
allowed_results: RI0 all markers; RI1 slots one/two; RI2 slot two
permitted_state_changes: Selected suffix destinations and completed IP/RI
cases.committed_prefix: Slots below entry RI are never replayed
cases.restart_action: Begin at exactly RI and finish with canonical RI zero

oracle.derivation: A literal suffix matrix defines the three expected register tuples

citation.anchor: Bundles are ordered from lowest to highest memory address

preconditions: No-stop groups cross translator and page boundaries
inputs: 5/6/16-bundle groups, automatic TCG-op segmentation, and a 0xff0/0x1000 group-entry overlay
expected_results: Split execution equals uninterrupted architectural execution
allowed_results: Exact destination, predicate, NaT, and terminal state
permitted_state_changes: Destinations selected by the ordered group
required_unchanged_state: Group-entry source values and their NaT pairing
cases.committed_prefix: Nonarchitectural boundaries add no commitment point
cases.restart_action: Translation segmentation does not alter restart state

oracle.derivation: Literal group-entry values and result markers define equivalence independently of translation segmentation
